pi_items.source: setup_task.sh defines OUTPUT_DIR=/home/ga/Documents/case_conference; export_result.sh references the same path at line 16; all 6 output files are cleaned and checked relative to this directory
export_result.sh line 17 defines stl_path = os.path.join(output_dir, 'cortical_bone.stl'); setup_task.sh cleans this file before task start
export_result.sh line 18 defines ply_path = os.path.join(output_dir, 'soft_tissue.ply'); setup_task.sh cleans this file before task start
export_result.sh line 19 defines anterior_path = os.path.join(output_dir, 'anterior_view.png')
export_result.sh line 20 defines lateral_path = os.path.join(output_dir, 'lateral_view.png')
export_result.sh line 21 defines cross_section_path = os.path.join(output_dir, 'cross_section.png')
export_result.sh line 22 defines inv3_path = os.path.join(output_dir, 'case_package.inv3')

Matches InVesalius 3 source code 'Compact Bone (Adult)' preset exactly (662-1988 HU) from invesalius/presets.py thresh_ct dictionary. Also consistent with published cortical bone segmentation threshold of 662 +/-29 HU (PubMed).

Matches InVesalius 3 source code 'Compact Bone (Adult)' preset exactly (662-1988 HU) from invesalius/presets.py thresh_ct dictionary. Upper bound excludes enamel (which starts at 1553 in InVesalius but has distinct higher range 1553-2850).
Matches InVesalius 3 source code 'Bone' preset exactly (226-3071 HU) from invesalius/presets.py. Also consistent with medical imaging standard for mineralized bone tissue lower bound.
Matches InVesalius 3 source code 'Bone' preset exactly (226-3071 HU). Value 3071 corresponds to maximum representable HU in standard 12-bit CT data (4096 - 1024 - 1).
Matches InVesalius 3 source code 'Soft Tissue' preset exactly (-700 to 225 HU) from invesalius/presets.py thresh_ct dictionary.

Matches InVesalius 3 source code 'Soft Tissue' preset exactly (-700 to 225 HU). Note: 225 is exactly one below the 'Bone' preset lower bound (226), ensuring no overlap between Soft Tissue and Bone presets.

Task requires 4 masks: Cortical Bone (662-1988), Full Bone (226-3071), Soft Tissue (-700-225), and Cancellous Bone (boolean subtraction: Full Bone minus Cortical Bone). This is consistent with the task description and case_specs.txt.

Task description specifies 'decimate it below 300,000 triangles'. This is a reasonable decimation target for 3D-printable cranial models; the cranioplasty_implant_pipeline task uses 400,000 as its limit. A full bone surface from 108-slice CT typically generates 50,000-500,000 triangles depending on threshold.

Reasonable minimum for a cranial cortical bone surface. The existing export_skull_stl task uses 10,000 as minimum for a full bone surface. Since cortical bone (662-1988 HU) captures less volume than full bone (226-3071 HU), a lower minimum of 5,000 is appropriate.
Consistent with cranioplasty_implant_pipeline which also uses min_ply_vertices=1000. A soft tissue surface from a cranial CT will have many more vertices than this minimum.

30 KB minimum. The anatomical_views_panoramic task uses 30 KB as its minimum (min_size_kb: 30). InVesalius screenshots of 3D models at 1920x1080 are typically 100-500 KB, so 30 KB is a reasonable lower bound that filters out blank/corrupt images.
Task description specifies 3 linear measurements: maximum anteroposterior diameter, maximum biparietal width, and one orbital dimension. Consistent with case_specs.txt.
Task description specifies one angular measurement of the cranial base angle. This is detected in export_result.sh via type field or 3-point heuristic in measurements.plist.

Consistent with cranioplasty_implant_pipeline (min_measurement_mm: 10.0). All expected cranial measurements (AP diameter ~170-200mm, biparietal width ~130-155mm, orbital dimensions ~24-49mm) are well above 10mm.

setup_invesalius.sh line 73 downloads 0051.zip from https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip and extracts to /opt/invesalius/sample_data/ct_cranium, symlinked to /home/ga/DICOM/ct_cranium. This is the official InVesalius v3.0 CT Cranium sample. Dataset contains 108 axial DICOM slices at 0.957x0.957mm in-plane resolution with 1.5mm slice thickness.

privileged_info_summary: This task uses the official InVesalius 3.0 CT Cranium sample dataset (0051.zip from GitHub releases), which is real medical imaging data (not synthetic). The dataset contains 108 axial DICOM slices at 0.957x0.957mm in-plane resolution and 1.5mm slice thickness. All HU thresholds correspond exactly to InVesalius built-in presets: 'Compact Bone (Adult)' = 662-1988 HU, 'Bone' = 226-3071 HU, 'Soft Tissue' = -700-225 HU. The task requires 4 masks (Cortical Bone, Full Bone, Soft Tissue, and Cancellous Bone via boolean subtraction of Cortical from Full). Two 3D surfaces must be generated: Cortical Bone (smoothed and decimated below 300,000 triangles, exported as cortical_bone.stl) and Soft Tissue (set semi-transparent, exported as soft_tissue.ply). Three linear measurements are required: maximum anteroposterior diameter (expected ~170-200mm), maximum biparietal width (expected ~130-155mm), and one orbital dimension (expected ~24-49mm). One angular measurement of the cranial base angle is required (normal adult range ~125-135 degrees, measured as nasion-sella-basion angle). Three screenshots are required: anterior_view.png (Norma Frontalis), lateral_view.png (Norma Lateralis), and cross_section.png (sagittal clipping plane showing internal bone structure). All PNGs must be distinct files (anti-gaming hash check). The complete project is saved as case_package.inv3, which is a gzip-compressed tarfile containing mask_*.plist files (with threshold_range), surface_*.plist files (with transparency), and measurements.plist. All outputs go to /home/ga/Documents/case_conference/.
